title: Ngrok Alternative
excerpt: I am trying to make my .js file available publicly for a Monday.com webhook. I have previously used ngrok to make this happen but ngrok’s free version has a time limit of 8 hours. Are their any ngrok alternatives that pr…

posts.author: cbacon (Caleb Bacon)
posts.cookedHtml: I am trying to make my .js file available publicly for a Monday.com webhook. I have previously used ngrok to make this happen but ngrok’s free version has a time limit of 8 hours. Are their any ngrok alternatives that provide no time limit and a static url to keep in the monday webhook?

posts.author: dipro (Dipro Bhowmik)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @cbacon!

ngrok is awesome for local testing, but it does have that time limitation on the free version. Instead of alternatives to ngrok, I’d recommend considering cloud hosting providers like AWS, GCP or Heroku. These services let you run your code on their machines, and take the hassle out of setting up your own server, making sure it’s accessible to the public web, etc etc.

If you’re looking for a free alternative to ngrok’s tunneling tech (and have a server at a public URL already), you could set up a connection between that server and your local machine using a reverse SSH tunnel. Here’s a guide how on how.

Hope that helps!

posts.author: cbacon (Caleb Bacon)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks @dipro. I’ll let you know how it goes.

posts.author: cbacon (Caleb Bacon)
posts.cookedHtml:
@dipro
Do I use Google Cloud Run for the webhook url or some other service at Google?

posts.author: dipro (Dipro Bhowmik)
posts.cookedHtml: I think GCP Run would work. But honestly, I’m not an expert with GCP – maybe someone else on the community can help here?

posts.author: freek-gcompany (Freek van de Griendt)
posts.cookedHtml: @cbacon We were thinking about using Cloud Functions on GCP but didn’t manage to get it working yet.

posts.author: kioleanu (Viorel Sfetea)
posts.cookedHtml: A quick alternative is localtunnel. Install it locally with npm install -g localtunnel and change your package.json to use localtunnel: instead "expose": "ngrok http 8301", use "expose": "lt --port 8301"
